checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2015 filing (comparative FY2014 balance sheet), AR

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2015 filing, comparative FY2014 balance sheet

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2015 filing, comparative cash flow statement (Q1 2014)

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss start=2014-01-01 end=2014-12-31 value=64371231.99 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations start=2014-01-01 end=2014-12-31 value=68495161.55

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or CostOfGoodsSold concept present in the provided series

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Revenues start=2012-01-01 end=2012-12-31 value=149118089.77 (a decline from 2011 value=155872736.58)
NetIncomeLoss start=2012-01-01 end=2012-12-31 value=46110463.48 (a decline from 2011 value=53147484.61)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: No AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivable or specific reserve concept present in the provided series
DeferredRevenueCurrent end=2013-12-31 value=12275275.66 vs end=2014-12-31 value=10527083.55 while Revenues grew from 182332054.07 (2013) to 194392809.53 (2014)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 balance sheet and income statement

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Repeated CORRESP/UPLOAD entries in late 2013 through April 2015 (e.g., UPLOAD 2014-11-12, CORRESP 2014-11-25, UPLOAD 2014-12-15, CORRESP 2014-12-29, UPLOAD 2015-01-15, CORRESP 2015-01-20, UPLOAD 2015-02-11, CORRESP 2015-02-24, UPLOAD 2015-03-25, CORRESP 2015-04-02, UPLOAD 2015-04-23) culminating in a 10-K/A on 2015-04-30

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Series covers Assets, AR, Intangibles, Goodwill, Liabilities, Debt, NetIncomeLoss, Revenues, OCF/ICF/FCF from 2008-2015 Q1 but omits InventoryNet, CostOfGoodsSold, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts, and detailed tax/reserve line items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature/aggressive revenue recognition or channel-stuffing in FY2014, evidenced by receivables growing ~31% while revenue grew only ~6.6%, resulting in revenue and AR being overstated relative to cash collected, with operating cash flow lagging net income growth.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss Q1 2015=26484183.28 (+5% YoY) while NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations Q1 2015=13935805.29, down ~37% from Q1 2014 value 22229602.92
rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2015 cash flow and income statement

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible capitalization of costs (e.g., promotional, deferred financing, or transaction costs) into 'Other Assets Noncurrent' rather than expensing, which would understate SG&A/operating expenses and overstate operating income/net income; consistent with the ~91% jump in this soft-asset line versus ~6.6% revenue growth in FY2014.

Anomalous mid-2014 reclassification of debt from non-current to current (and reversal one quarter later) suggests possible covenant-trigger classification issues or error/correction in debt classification, which combined with the recurring pattern of 10-K/A amendments and SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence around FY2014-2015 filings, raises concern about classification/disclosure misstatements requiring subsequent correction.

rationale_evidence.quote: LiabilitiesCurrent end=2014-06-30 value=308530198.91 (versus end=2014-03-31 value=57340532.66 and end=2014-09-30 value=60102743.63)
LongTermDebtCurrent end=2014-06-30 value=280734450.09 (versus end=2014-03-31 value=25814071.07 and end=2014-09-30 value=25760546.39)
10-K filed 2015-03-02 followed by 10-K/A filed 2015-04-30, preceded by multiple CORRESP/UPLOAD filings (2015-01-15 through 2015-04-23)

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew ~31% YoY (FY2013→FY2014) while revenue grew only ~6.6%, indicating potential revenue recognition or collections issues
Operating cash flow (continuing operations) fell ~37% YoY in Q1 2015 while net income rose ~5%, indicating deteriorating cash quality of earnings
'Other Assets Noncurrent' nearly doubled (+91%) in FY2014 versus modest revenue growth, suggesting possible cost capitalization

Recurring pattern of 10-K/A amendments filed ~2 months after each 10-K (2011-2015) combined with frequent SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence clustered around the FY2014 10-K and 10-K/A, suggesting active SEC scrutiny
Anomalous one-quarter spike and reversal in current liabilities/current portion of long-term debt in mid-2014, suggesting classification volatility
